meaning: adj.adv.飘浮的,在海上的,传播的
examples: The ancients conceived the earth as afloat in water.
We kept her afloat for a couple of hours by bailing frantically.
Icebergs afloat today are made of snowflakes that have fallen over long ages of time.
No longer can the objective merely be to keep the Haitian people afloat with humanitarian aid.
In 2011, the company ran a trial with 20-ft pens set afloat off Hawaii.
